quotes.verbatim_quote:
The use of enharmonic tones is justifiable whenever the student desires. The enharmonic tone does
not change the sovwnd of the tone but rather the spelling, which may allow the student to use the
material related to whole tone scales. For reference, the enharmonic tones are: C-sharp and D-flat:
D-sharp and E-flat; E-sharp and F; F-sharp and G-flat; G-sharp and A-flat; A-sharp and B-flat; B-
sharp and C; F-flat and E: C-flat and B.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Establishes the rule that enharmonic substitution is permitted and lists all enharmonic pairs, which is operationally necessary for extracting triads and seventh chords from the whole-tone scale chart without being tripped up by spelling.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
By extracting the tones No. 1-3-5 from any whole tone scale, we arrive at the Augmented Triad on
that particular root.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Gives the defining extraction formula for the Augmented Triad, making it the single operative rule students apply to the whole-tone scale chart.

quotes.topic: Augmented Triad apparent six-tone span explained
quotes.verbatim_quote:
The student is cautioned vof to be misled into thinking the augmented
triad has six tones.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Issues the explicit corrective warning against a predictable misreading caused by enharmonic respelling in the chart, preventing a conceptual error about triad structure.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The correct spelling for the E Augmented Triad should be E-G-sharp-B-sharp. The B-sharp, how-
ever, was replaced with the enharmonic note C in the E whole tone scale. This is why the illu-
strated E Augmented Triad appears to have six tones from the root to the top tone, instead of fire.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Traces exactly why the chart produces an apparent six-tone span and supplies the theoretically correct spelling, anchoring the student's understanding of enharmonic substitution in a concrete example.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
By extracting tones No. 1-3-5-6 from a whole ione scale, we arrive at the Augmented Seventh chord
on that particular root.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Provides the parallel extraction formula for the Augmented Seventh Chord, the chapter's second core derivation rule.

quotes.topic: Shared triad and scale between chords
quotes.verbatim_quote:
Note that both chords contain the same triad, E-G-sharp-C, and both chords use the same scale, the
E whole tone scale, to establish their sound.
quotes.why_load_bearing: States the structural relationship between the Augmented Triad and Augmented Seventh Chord — same parent triad, same parent scale — giving students a unifying principle for voice-leading and substitution.
